titleStmt.title: The Secretary of State to the Ambassador in the Soviet Union ( (Harriman))

div.head: The Secretary of State to the Ambassador in the Soviet Union ( Harriman )
div.p:
1676. For your information, a Soviet Embassy note of July 6Not printed. indicates a desire to confer “the highest Soviet military orders” on
Lt. General Omar Bradley,Commanding General, 1st U. S. Army, Normandy campaign. Lt. General Leonard Gerow,Commander, U. S. Forces in England. Lt. General Mark Clark,Commander, 5th U.S. Army in Italy. and Maj. General James CollinsIn a note from the Secretary of State of July 24, 1944, approving the acceptance of the offered awards, the Soviet Chargé, Alexander Nikolayevich Kapustin, was advised of a possible error; it was presumed that Maj. Gen. J. Lawton Collins, commanding the VII Corps in France, was intended as the recipient of an award rather than Maj. Gen. James L. Collins, who was on duty in the United States (093.612/7–1844). in connection with successful Allied operations in France and Italy. The approval of the War Department is being requested in accordance with former practice.
With reference to your 1480, April 28,Not printed. has the Soviet Government been informed of our new policy regarding decorations, particularly paragraph (a) of Department’s 837, April 8?
